# gssrdoc ![](reference/figures/gssrdoc.png)

Documentation for the General Social Survey Cumulative Data (1972-2024,
R3) packaged for easy use in R. This package is a companion to the
[`gssr`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssr) package. I recommend you
install both of them.

## Installation

The relatively large number of variables documented in
[gssrdoc](https://github.com/kjhealy/gssrdoc) means it is not suitable
for hosting on [CRAN](https://cran.r-project.org/), the core R package
repository. The same is true of the
[`gssr`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssr) package.

### Install via R-Universe

My [R Universe](https://kjhealy.r-universe.dev/) provides binary
packages for [gssrdoc](https://github.com/kjhealy/gssrdoc) and
[`gssr`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssr). To install both packages, copy
and paste the following code to the R console:

``` r
# Install 'gssrdoc' from 'ropensci' universe
install.packages('gssrdoc', repos =
  c('https://kjhealy.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))

# Also recommended: install 'gssr' as well
install.packages('gssr', repos =
  c('https://kjhealy.r-universe.dev', 'https://cloud.r-project.org'))
```

Because the packages have dependencies that are on CRAN, we add CRAN as
well as the R Universe to the `repos` argument. These binary packages
will install much faster than building the package from source. Plus,
you can use
[`install.packages()`](https://rdrr.io/r/utils/install.packages.html)
directly.

## Install direct from GitHub

You can also install gssrdoc from
[GitHub](https://github.com/kjhealy/gssrdoc) with:

``` r
remotes::install_github("kjhealy/gssrdoc")
```

## Integrated Help

[gssrdoc](https://github.com/kjhealy/gssrdoc) provides documentation for
all GSS variables in the cumulative data file via R’s help system. You
can browse variables by name in the package’s help file or type `?`
followed by the name of the variable at the console to get a standard R
help page containing information on the variable, the values it takes,
and the years and ballots it is available for. This makes it much easier
to check GSS documentation while using R in an IDE such as RStudio,
VSCode, or Positron. The help page for each variable links to the [GSS
Data Explorer](https://gssdataexplorer.norc.org) page for that variable.
The “See Also” section at the bottom of the help page incorporates the
GSS’s own cross-referencing by module. The [documentation website for
the packge](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc) has a full listing of the
variables and a topical index organized by survey module and the GSS’s
own subject tags.

![Example help page](reference/figures/readme_sshot.png)

Information about the variables is also contained in the `gss_doc`
object:

``` r
library(tibble)
library(gssrdoc)

> gss_doc
# A tibble: 6,942 × 10
   variable description                           question                                          value_labels var_yrtab yrballot_df module_df subject_df norc_id norc_url
   <chr>    <chr>                                 <chr>                                             <chr>        <list>    <list>      <list>    <list>       <int> <chr>   
 1 year     GSS year for this respondent          "GSS year"                                        "[NA(d)] do… <chr [1]> <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         1 https:/…
 2 id       Respondent id number                  "Respondent id number"                            ""           <chr [1]> <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         2 https:/…
 3 wrkstat  labor force status                    "Last week were you working full time, part time… "[1] workin… <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         3 https:/…
 4 hrs1     number of hours worked last week      "Last week were you working full time, part time… "[89] 89+ h… <chr [1]> <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         4 https:/…
 5 hrs2     number of hours usually work a week   "Last week were you working full time, part time… "[89] 89+ h… <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         5 https:/…
 6 evwork   ever work as long as one year         "Last week were you working full time, part time… "[1] yes / … <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         6 https:/…
 7 occ      R's census occupation code (1970)     "A. What kind of work do you (did you normally) … "[NA(d)] do… <chr [1]> <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         7 https:/…
 8 prestige r's occupational prestige score(1970) "A. What kind of work do you (did you normally) … "[NA(d)] do… <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         8 https:/…
 9 wrkslf   r self-emp or works for somebody      "A. What kind of work do you (did you normally) … "[1] self-e… <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>         9 https:/…
10 wrkgovt  govt or private employee              "A. What kind of work do you (did you normally) … "[1] govern… <tibble>  <tibble>    <tibble>  <tibble>        10 https:/…
# ℹ 6,932 more rows
# ℹ Use `print(n = ...)` to see more rows
```

## Official GSS Documentation

## Official GSS Documentation

The [GSS Documentation
Page](https://gss.norc.org/us/en/gss/get-documentation.html) contains
links to extensive technical documentation for the survey, including
[Release Notes for the 1972-2024 R3 Cumulative
File](https://gss.norc.org/content/dam/gss/get-documentation/pdf/codebook/GSS%202024%20Codebook%20R3.pdf).

## Further details

The package is documented at <http://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/>. The
GSS homepage is at <http://gss.norc.org/>. While
[gssrdoc](https://github.com/kjhealy/gssrdoc) package incorporates the
publicly-available GSS cumulative data file, this package is not
associated with or endorsed by the National Opinion Research Center or
the General Social Survey.

# Package index

## Objects

Tibble documenting the cumulative data file.

- [`gss_doc`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/gss_doc.md) :
  Data Dictionary for the GSS Cumulative Data File 1972-2024 Release 3

## Variables

GSS Variables in the cumulative data file.

### A

- [`abany`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abany.md) :
  Abortion if woman wants for any reason
- [`abanyg`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abanyg.md) :
  Abortion if woman wants for any reason (grid on web)
- [`abbelief`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abbelief.md)
  : Pro-choice vs pro-life
- [`abcare`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abcare.md) :
  How concerned is r about abortion
- [`abchoose`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abchoose.md)
  : Should a woman get an abortion for any reason
- [`abcon1`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abcon1.md) :
  1st argument against abortion
- [`abcon2`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abcon2.md) :
  2nd argument against abortion
- [`abcon3`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abcon3.md) :
  3rd argument against abortion
- [`abdefct1`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abdefct1.md)
  : Law should allow abortion for birth defects
- [`abdefctw`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abdefctw.md)
  : Wrong for woman to get abortion for birth defects
- [`abdefect`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abdefect.md)
  : Strong chance of serious defect
- [`abdefectg`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abdefectg.md)
  : Strong chance of serious defect (grid on web)
- [`abfelegl`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abfelegl.md)
  : Women only: women should be able to have legal abortions
- [`abfirm`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abfirm.md) :
  How firm is r's opinion on abortion
- [`abgender`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abgender.md)
  : Aboriton legal for gender selection
- [`abhave1`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhave1.md) :
  Have abortion if probable defect
- [`abhave2`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhave2.md) :
  Have abortion if health endangered
- [`abhave3`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhave3.md) :
  Have abortion if couldn't afford child
- [`abhelp1`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhelp1.md) :
  R would help with arrangements for abortion
- [`abhelp2`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhelp2.md) :
  R would help with paying for abortion
- [`abhelp3`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhelp3.md) :
  R would help with paying for abortion-related other costs
- [`abhelp4`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhelp4.md) :
  R would help with emotional support for abortion
- [`abhlth`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhlth.md) :
  Woman's health seriously endangered
- [`abhlthg`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abhlthg.md) :
  Woman's health seriously endangered (grid on web)
- [`abimp`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abimp.md) :
  Importance of abortion issue to r
- [`abinfo`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abinfo.md) :
  How much info does r have on abortion
- [`abinspay`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abinspay.md)
  : Should people be able to use health insurance for abortion - ver y
- [`ablegal`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/ablegal.md) :
  Should abortion ever be legal
- [`abmedgov1`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abmedgov1.md)
  : Woman and doctor or govt should decide what info needed for
  abortion - ver x
- [`abmedgov2`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abmedgov2.md)
  : Woman and doctor or govt should decide what info needed for
  childbirth - ver y
- [`abmelegl`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abmelegl.md)
  : Men only: women should be able to have legal abortions
- [`abmoral`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abmoral.md) :
  R has a moral opposition to abortion
- [`abnomore`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abnomore.md)
  : Married–wants no more children
- [`abnomoreg`](https://kjhealy.github.io/gssrdoc/reference/abnomoreg.md)
  : Married–wants no more children (grid on web)

  Heard supreme court decision on abortion

  Past 12 months r web to learn abortion

  Low income–cant afford more children
